_limbo)
test_limbo_cleanup
ThreadTests.test_limbo_cleanupc
-caN
if 1:
            import ctypes, sys, time, _thread
            # This lock is used as a simple event variable.
            ready = _thread.allocate_lock()
            ready.acquire()
            # Module globals are cleared before __del__ is run
            # So we save the functions in class dict
            class C:
                ensure = ctypes.pythonapi.PyGILState_Ensure
                release = ctypes.pythonapi.PyGILState_Release
                def __del__(self):
                    state = self.ensure()
                    self.release(state)
            def waitingThread():
                x = C()
                ready.release()
                time.sleep(100)
            _thread.start_new_thread(waitingThread, ())
            ready.acquire()  # Be sure the other thread is waiting.
            sys.exit(42)
            
errr
test_finalize_running_thread#
z(ThreadTests.test_finalize_running_threadc
if 1:
            import sys, threading
            # A deadlock-killer, to prevent the
            # testsuite to hang forever
            def killer():
                import os, time
                time.sleep(2)
                print('program blocked; aborting')
                os._exit(2)
            t = threading.Thread(target=killer)
            t.daemon = True
            t.start()
            # This is the trace function
            def func(frame, event, arg):
                threading.current_thread()
                return func
            sys.settrace(func)
            )
test_finalize_with_traceD
z$ThreadTests.test_finalize_with_tracec
if 1:
                import threading
                from time import sleep
                def child():
                    sleep(1)
                    # As a non-daemon thread we SHOULD wake up and nothing
                    # should be torn down yet
                    print("Woke up, sleep function is:", sleep)
                threading.Thread(target=child).start()
                raise SystemExit
            s5

test_daemon_param
ThreadTests.test_daemon_param
fork
needs os.fork()c
            import atexit
            import os
            import sys
            from test.support import wait_process
            # Import the threading module to register its "at fork" callback
            import threading
            def exit_handler():
                pid = os.fork()
                if not pid:
                    print("child process ok", file=sys.stderr, flush=True)
                    # child process
                    sys.exit()
                else:
                    wait_process(pid, exitcode=0)
            # exit_handler() will be called after threading._shutdown()
            atexit.register(exit_handler)
        r
child process ok)
textwrap
dedentr
rstrip
code
test_fork_at_exit
ThreadTests.test_fork_at_exitz
test needs fork()c
if 1:
            import _thread, threading, os, time
            def background_thread(evt):
                # Creates and registers the _DummyThread instance
                threading.current_thread()
                evt.set()
                time.sleep(10)
            evt = threading.Event()
            _thread.start_new_thread(background_thread, (evt,))
            evt.wait()
            assert threading.active_count() == 2, threading.active_count()
            if os.fork() == 0:
                assert threading.active_count() == 1, threading.active_count()
                os._exit(0)
            else:
                os.wait()
        r

test needs os.fork()
waitpidz
test needs os.waitpid()c
if 1:
            import os, threading
            from test import support
            pid = os.fork()
            if pid == 0:
                main = threading.main_thread()
                print(main.name)
                print(main.ident == threading.current_thread().ident)
                print(main.ident == threading.get_ident())
            else:
                support.wait_process(pid, exitcode=0)
        r
MainThread
True

test_main_thread_after_fork
z'ThreadTests.test_main_thread_after_fork
due to known OS bugc
if 1:
            import os, threading, sys
            from test import support
            def f():
                pid = os.fork()
                if pid == 0:
                    main = threading.main_thread()
                    print(main.name)
                    print(main.ident == threading.current_thread().ident)
                    print(main.ident == threading.get_ident())
                    # stdout is fully buffered because not a tty,
                    # we have to flush before exit.
                    sys.stdout.flush()
                else:
                    support.wait_process(pid, exitcode=0)
            th = threading.Thread(target=f)
            th.start()
            th.join()
        r
Thread-1
True
True
/test_main_thread_after_fork_from_nonmain_thread*
z;ThreadTests.test_main_thread_after_fork_from_nonmain_threadc
if 1:
            import gc, threading
            main_thread = threading.current_thread()
            assert main_thread is threading.main_thread()  # sanity check
            class RefCycle:
                def __init__(self):
                    self.cycle = self
                def __del__(self):
                    print("GC:",
                          threading.current_thread() is main_thread,
                          threading.main_thread() is main_thread,
                          threading.enumerate() == [main_thread])
            RefCycle()
            gc.collect()  # sanity check
            x = RefCycle()
        r
GC: True True True
splitlinesr
 test_main_thread_during_shutdownH
z,ThreadTests.test_main_thread_during_shutdownc
if 1:
            import os
            import threading
            import time
            import random
            def random_sleep():
                seconds = random.random() * 0.010
                time.sleep(seconds)
            class Sleeper:
                def __del__(self):
                    random_sleep()
            tls = threading.local()
            def f():
                # Sleep a bit so that the thread is still running when
                # Py_Finalize() is called.
                random_sleep()
                tls.x = Sleeper()
                random_sleep()
            threading.Thread(target=f).start()
            random_sleep()
        r

if 1:
            import threading
            class Atexit:
                def __del__(self):
                    print("thread_dict.atexit = %r" % thread_dict.atexit)
            thread_dict = threading.local()
            thread_dict.atexit = "value"
            atexit = Atexit()
        s
thread_dict.atexit = 'value')
test_locals_at_exit

noop,
z0ThreadTests.test_leak_without_join.<locals>.noopr
test_leak_without_join)
z"ThreadTests.test_leak_without_joinc
            import _thread
            import sys
            event = _thread.allocate_lock()
            event.acquire()
            def import_threading():
                import threading
                event.release()
            if 'threading' in sys.modules:
                raise Exception('threading is already imported')
            _thread.start_new_thread(import_threading, ())
            # wait until the threading module is imported
            event.acquire()
            event.release()
            if 'threading' not in sys.modules:
                raise Exception('threading is not imported')
            # don't wait until the thread completes
        r
test_import_from_another_thread1

platforms_to_skipr
ThreadJoinOnShutdownc
if 1:
            import sys, os, time, threading
            # a thread, which waits for the main program to terminate
            def joiningfunc(mainthread):
                mainthread.join()
                print('end of thread')
                # stdout is fully buffered because not a tty, we have to flush
                # before exit.
                sys.stdout.flush()
        
end of main
end of thread
scriptr
_run_and_joinU
z"ThreadJoinOnShutdown._run_and_joinc
if 1:
            import os
            t = threading.Thread(target=joiningfunc,
                                 args=(threading.current_thread(),))
            t.start()
            time.sleep(0.1)
            print('end of main')
            
test_1_join_on_shutdownf
z,ThreadJoinOnShutdown.test_1_join_on_shutdownr
if 1:
            from test import support
            childpid = os.fork()
            if childpid != 0:
                # parent process
                support.wait_process(childpid, exitcode=0)
                sys.exit(0)
            # child process
            t = threading.Thread(target=joiningfunc,
                                 args=(threading.current_thread(),))
            t.start()
            print('end of main')
            r
test_2_join_in_forked_processr
z2ThreadJoinOnShutdown.test_2_join_in_forked_processc
if 1:
            from test import support
            main_thread = threading.current_thread()
            def worker():
                childpid = os.fork()
                if childpid != 0:
                    # parent process
                    support.wait_process(childpid, exitcode=0)
                    sys.exit(0)
                # child process
                t = threading.Thread(target=joiningfunc,
                                     args=(main_thread,))
                print('end of main')
                t.start()
                t.join() # Should not block: main_thread is already stopped
            w = threading.Thread(target=worker)
            w.start()
            r
!test_3_join_in_forked_from_thread
z6ThreadJoinOnShutdown.test_3_join_in_forked_from_threadc
if True:
            import os
            import random
            import sys
            import time
            import threading
            thread_has_run = set()
            def random_io():
                '''Loop for a while sleeping random tiny amounts and doing some I/O.'''
                while True:
                    with open(os.__file__, 'rb') as in_f:
                        stuff = in_f.read(200)
                        with open(os.devnull, 'wb') as null_f:
                            null_f.write(stuff)
                            time.sleep(random.random() / 1995)
                    thread_has_run.add(threading.current_thread())
            def main():
                count = 0
                for _ in range(40):
                    new_thread = threading.Thread(target=random_io)
                    new_thread.daemon = True
                    new_thread.start()
                    count += 1
                while len(thread_has_run) < count:
                    time.sleep(0.001)
                # Trigger process shutdown
                sys.exit(0)
            main()
            r
test_4_daemon_threads
z*ThreadJoinOnShutdown.test_4_daemon_threadsc

piper
closer@
SubinterpThreadingTests.pipec
            import os
            import random
            import threading
            import time
            def random_sleep():
                seconds = random.random() * 0.010
                time.sleep(seconds)
            def f():
                # Sleep a bit so that the thread is still running when
                # Py_EndInterpreter is called.
                random_sleep()
                os.write(%d, b"x")
            threading.Thread(target=f).start()
            random_sleep()
        r
run_in_subinterprC
read
test_threads_join
z)SubinterpThreadingTests.test_threads_joinc
            import os
            import random
            import threading
            import time
            def random_sleep():
                seconds = random.random() * 0.010
                time.sleep(seconds)
            class Sleeper:
                def __del__(self):
                    random_sleep()
            tls = threading.local()
            def f():
                # Sleep a bit so that the thread is still running when
                # Py_EndInterpreter is called.
                random_sleep()
                tls.x = Sleeper()
                os.write(%d, b"x")
            threading.Thread(target=f).start()
            random_sleep()
        r

            import os
            import threading
            import time
            def f():
                # Make sure the daemon thread is still running when
                # Py_EndInterpreter is called.
                time.sleep(zJ)
            threading.Thread(target=f, daemon=True).start()
            z[if 1:

)	Na
if True:
            import threading
            def recurse():
                return recurse()
            def outer():
                try:
                    recurse()
                except RecursionError:
                    pass
            w = threading.Thread(target=outer)
            w.start()
            w.join()
            print('end of main thread')
            z

test_recursion_limit{
z,ThreadingExceptionTests.test_recursion_limitc
if True:
            import threading
            import time
            running = False
            def run():
                global running
                running = True
                while running:
                    time.sleep(0.01)
                1/0
            t = threading.Thread(target=run)
            t.start()
            while not running:
                time.sleep(0.01)
            running = False
            t.join()
            r
Exception in thread
"Traceback (most recent call last):

test_print_exception
z,ThreadingExceptionTests.test_print_exceptionc
if True:
            import sys
            import threading
            import time
            running = False
            def run():
                global running
                running = True
                while running:
                    time.sleep(0.01)
                1/0
            t = threading.Thread(target=run)
            t.start()
            while not running:
                time.sleep(0.01)
            sys.stderr = None
            running = False
            t.join()
            r
%test_print_exception_stderr_is_none_1
z=ThreadingExceptionTests.test_print_exception_stderr_is_none_1c
if True:
            import sys
            import threading
            import time
            running = False
            def run():
                global running
                running = True
                while running:
                    time.sleep(0.01)
                1/0
            sys.stderr = None
            t = threading.Thread(target=run)
            t.start()
            while not running:
                time.sleep(0.01)
            running = False
            t.join()
            r

AtexitTestsc
if True:
            import threading
            def run_last():
                print('parrot')
            threading._register_atexit(run_last)
        s
parrot)
test_atexit_output
AtexitTests.test_atexit_outputc
if True:
            import threading
            from unittest.mock import Mock
            mock = Mock()
            threading._register_atexit(mock)
            mock.assert_not_called()
            # force early shutdown to ensure it was called once
            threading._shutdown()
            mock.assert_called_once()
        r
test_atexit_called_once
z#AtexitTests.test_atexit_called_oncec
if True:
            import threading
            def func():
                pass
            def run_last():
                threading._register_atexit(func)
            threading._register_atexit(run_last)
        z2RuntimeError: can't register atexit after shutdown)
